findings.reachability: Reachable by any caller of post_assert or redirect_assert that passes options.ignore_signature=true; the entire assertion signature check is unconditionally skipped and the attacker-supplied XML is trusted as-is.
findings.justification: Line 589: `if (ignore_signature) { return cb_wf(null, (new xmldom.DOMParser()).parseFromString(result)); }` — when this flag is set, a forged or replayed SAML assertion passes without any cryptographic check.

detection_pattern.description: Detects SAML libraries that expose an ignore_signature flag; when set to true this completely disables signature verification and allows arbitrary assertion forgery.

findings.reachability: Reachable by any unauthenticated attacker who can craft a SAML response; the signed outer envelope may differ from the unsigned inner assertion accepted by the application.
findings.justification: The code calls check_saml_signature on both the extracted assertion string and the full saml_response_str (line 597); get_signed_data returns all signed references and the loop accepts the first signed_dom that contains a saml:Assertion (line 609). An attacker can craft a response where the outer signed envelope contains a benign assertion but the actual assertion parsed from the response is unsigned/modified — classic XML Signature Wrapping.

detection_pattern.section_name: SAML: XML Signature Wrapping via getSignedReferences
detection_pattern.description: Detects SAML code that uses getSignedReferences without verifying the signed element IS the assertion consumed downstream; enables signature wrapping attacks where a valid signature on a dummy element co-exists with an unsigned malicious assertion.

findings.reachability: Reachable if the IdP (or a MITM in allow_unencrypted mode) can supply arbitrary attribute Names in the SAML assertion; `assertion_attributes[attribute_name]` sets a key directly on a plain object without sanitisation.
findings.justification: Line 480: `assertion_attributes[attribute_name] = ...` where `attribute_name` is taken verbatim from the XML attribute Name value. An attribute named `__proto__` or `constructor` would pollute the object prototype chain for downstream code.

detection_pattern.section_name: Prototype Pollution: unchecked XML attribute key assignment
detection_pattern.description: Detects direct property assignment using XML-derived keys without hasOwnProperty guard or null-prototype objects; enables prototype pollution when an attacker controls the key.

findings.location: add_namespaces_to_child_assertions / line 523; parse_authn_response / line 807; check_saml_signature / line 353
findings.reachability: Reachable by any attacker who can supply a crafted SAML response (unauthenticated in IdP-initiated flows); depends on @xmldom/xmldom version's default entity-expansion behaviour.
findings.justification: Multiple DOMParser.parseFromString calls (lines 353, 523, 607, 807) operate on attacker-controlled XML. Older versions of @xmldom/xmldom expand external entities by default; if the deployed version does not explicitly disable entity expansion, XXE is possible leading to SSRF or file read.

detection_pattern.description: Detects DOMParser usage on external input without explicit entity-expansion hardening; relevant for xmldom-based parsers that historically expanded entities by default.

findings.reachability: Reachable when sp_audience is configured as a RegExp (a supported configuration path documented by line 652's _.isRegExp check); an overly-broad pattern allows assertions intended for other SPs to be accepted.
findings.justification: Line 652: `_.isRegExp(sp_audience) && sp_audience.test(audienceValue)` — if the application operator passes a broad regex like /.*/ or /example\.com/ (instead of anchored /^https:\/\/example\.com\/sp$/), a malicious IdP could include any audience URI and bypass the restriction.

detection_pattern.description: Detects SAML audience validation that allows RegExp matching; if the regex is not anchored or is overly broad, a malicious assertion with a crafted audience URI satisfies the check.

findings.reachability: Reachable when allow_unencrypted_assertion is true (a configurable default exposed via ServiceProvider shared_options); an attacker can strip the EncryptedAssertion wrapper and supply a plaintext assertion that bypasses decryption-based access controls.
findings.justification: Lines 577-584: when `allow_unencrypted` is true and the error is 'Expected 1 EncryptedAssertion; found 0', the code falls back to extracting a plain saml:Assertion from the response without any additional check that the assertion was originally intended to be unencrypted by the IdP.

detection_pattern.description: Detects SAML implementations that fall back to accepting plaintext assertions when decryption fails; enables downgrade attacks where an attacker strips encryption and replays a forged assertion.
